tests.name: activate snapshots every target before any write (three existing, one not-yet-existing)
activate aborts before any apply-phase write when a snapshot copy fails
activate emits a manifest enumerating every applied target with path/existed_before/snapshot_path/new_hash
activate aborts with zero writes when a target path is outside the declared allowlist
activate rejects an unsafe allowedPaths entry (dot/dotdot/traversal) instead of silently permitting everything
activate attaches a partial manifest to the thrown error when a later target fails mid-apply
isMainModule is true only when the module URL matches argv[1] resolved to a file URL
isMainModule is false when argv[1] points at a different file
isMainModule is false when argv[1] is undefined
isMainModule defaults the missing-argv1 case to the empty-string file URL, not an arbitrary string
runCli prints usage and returns exit code 2 when required args are missing
runCli runs activation end-to-end, writes stdout and --manifest-out, and returns 0
runCli writes the partial manifest to --manifest-out and returns 1 when activation fails partway through
activate CLI top-level entrypoint (in-process) runs the CLI and sets exitCode when argv[1] resolves to this module
activate CLI top-level entrypoint (in-process) does not run the CLI when argv[1] resolves to a different module
activate CLI entrypoint (subprocess) exits 2 with a usage message when required args are missing
Phase A scope guard permits src paths and the exact release-manifest exception
Phase A scope guard rejects every other implementation path
Phase A scope guard rejects traversal disguised beneath src
Phase A scope guard normalizes backslash separators to forward slashes before the src/ check
Phase A scope guard permits a declared allowedPaths entry and still rejects everything else outside src/
Phase A scope guard treats an allowedPaths entry as a directory prefix, permitting nested paths beneath it
Phase A scope guard keeps the zero-argument default behavior unchanged when allowedPaths is omitted
Phase A scope guard defaults allowedPaths to an empty array, not a permissive placeholder
Phase A scope guard rejects an unsafe allowedPaths entry "" instead of silently permitting everything
Phase A scope guard rejects an unsafe allowedPaths entry "." instead of silently permitting everything
Phase A scope guard rejects an unsafe allowedPaths entry ".." instead of silently permitting everything
Phase A scope guard rejects an unsafe allowedPaths entry "../escape" instead of silently permitting everything
Phase A scope guard normalizes backslash separators in allowedPaths entries too, not just in the checked paths
isMainModule is true only when the module URL matches argv[1] resolved to a file URL
isMainModule is false when argv[1] points at a different file
isMainModule is false when argv[1] is undefined
isMainModule defaults the missing-argv1 case to the empty-string file URL, not an arbitrary string
Phase A scope guard top-level CLI guard (in-process) runs the CLI side effect when argv[1] resolves to this module
Phase A scope guard top-level CLI guard (in-process) sets no exit code when argv[1] resolves to this module but every path is in scope
Phase A scope guard top-level CLI guard (in-process) does not run the CLI side effect when argv[1] resolves to a different module
Phase A scope guard CLI entrypoint prints violations to stderr and sets a non-zero exit code when run as the main module
Phase A scope guard CLI entrypoint joins multiple violations with a newline separator
Phase A scope guard CLI entrypoint prints nothing and exits 0 when all given paths are in scope
Phase A scope guard CLI entrypoint does not run the CLI block when imported as a module rather than executed
Phase A golden fixtures discovers every required case with valid normalized diagnostics
rollback produces an empty diff for both existed-before and did-not-exist-before targets
rollback refuses to restore (aborts before any write) when a target diverged from the recorded post-activation state
rollback detects a mismatch via injected divergence and names the path
rollback post-restore verification independently detects a mismatch even when the pre-restore check passes
isMainModule is true only when the module URL matches argv[1] resolved to a file URL
isMainModule is false when argv[1] points at a different file
isMainModule is false when argv[1] is undefined
isMainModule defaults the missing-argv1 case to the empty-string file URL, not an arbitrary string
runCli prints usage and returns exit code 2 when required args are missing
runCli runs rollback end-to-end and returns 0 on success
runCli returns 1 and writes an error message when rollback throws
rollback CLI top-level entrypoint (in-process) runs the CLI and sets exitCode when argv[1] resolves to this module
rollback CLI top-level entrypoint (in-process) does not run the CLI when argv[1] resolves to a different module
rollback CLI entrypoint (subprocess) exits 2 with a usage message when required args are missing
